summary: Assign the correct type to each volume in the model. In PoligonSoft, Casting volumes correspond to the casting itself, while Mold volumes correspond to the mold and auxiliary process elements.
actions:
Switch to the Modes tab and select Volume Types to review how PoligonSoft classified the bodies after importing the mesh.
Review the model colors: red elements correspond to the Casting type and blue elements correspond to the Mold type.
Use the auxiliary visibility buttons Casting Surfaces and Mold Surfaces to show or hide the Casting and Mold elements.
Double-click the volume you want to edit to open the Volume Parameters window.
Select the correct volume type: Casting for the casting body or Mold for the mold elements.
Assign a clear name to the volume to make the following steps easier.
Press OK to confirm the changes.
In the warning window, select Yes to recalculate the boundary indices. This topic will be explained in more detail in the lesson about heat transfer parameters.
Repeat the procedure until all elements correspond to the correct volume type.
expected: All volumes will be correctly classified as Casting or Mold. Each volume will also have a clear name, making it easier to assign materials, heat transfer parameters, and boundary conditions in the following lessons.
